claim: Delegation of the steward's personal-signature duty to an agent shall be valid ONLY when granted EXPLICITLY -- per-case or for a declared campaign in advance -- never implied or standing by default.
why: M5, REPLACES split into R-trust-anchor-mechanism + R-trust-anchor-delegation-explicit-only per atomicity discipline (R-requirement-claim-is-atomic). Steward verdict 2026-07-02 (verbatim): «человек обязан сам подписать, если только явно не делегирует это в каждом случае или на кампанию вперед» (English: 'the human is obliged to sign personally, unless he explicitly delegates it, either per-case or for a campaign in advance'). This atom lands the EXCEPTION half: delegation is not the default and is not implicit from e.g. an agent being given repo write access -- it requires an explicit steward grant, scoped either to one case or to a named campaign declared in advance. RETIRED MECHANISM (steward triage decision B1, 2026-07-09, verbatim: «снести старую jsonl-систему делегаций полностью»): the original mechanism -- domains/hotam-spec-self/delegations.jsonl, a durable committed append-only JSONL ledger written by tools/record_delegation.py, cross-checked against Conflict.lifecycle markers by tests/test_delegation_marker_honesty.py -- has been torn down completely. The seed record DEL-1 is preserved verbatim as inert historical record in docs/development/DELEGATIONS-HISTORY.md and in the Conflict's own Signoff.instrument="DEL-1" field (R-signoff-preserved-in-substrate), no longer mechanically cross-checked. NOT YET IMPLEMENTED: this claim is to be upheld by the file-based mechanism R-delegation-is-a-file established for task hand-offs -- a delegation is a versioned delegations/DG-<n>.md file, created and closed only via a delegate command, with git history as the audit trail. Neither the delegate tool nor the delegations/ file format exists: `delegate` is not registered in methodology.Tools at all (not even Planned), and no delegations/ directory exists. The steward's decision principle still stands: delegation requires explicit, scoped, advance grant -- never implied from write access. When the delegate tool is implemented, the file-based mechanism (delegations/DG-<n>.md, created/closed via a hotam delegate command) should implement this. PROSE (not ENFORCED): no enforcer exists (proven historically by tests/test_tool_delegate.py).
